name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value strictly greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize should always be a multiple of b; failing this means alignment is broken

name: aligned_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_broken(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If a is already aligned to b, align_to_usize should be the identity; disagreement indicates inconsistency

name: align_gap_exceeds_modulus
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_gap_exceeds_modulus(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
{
}
reason: The distance from a to its aligned-down value must be less than b; a gap >= b means a full unit was lost

name: zero_never_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_zero_never_aligned(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every nonzero value, so it must always be considered aligned; denying this is unsound
